content.full_text:
Re
Farn Polrucal PnacrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
November 19,2003
Trent J. Benedetti, Treasurer
Benedetti & Associates
2151 So. College Drive, Suite 101
Santa Maria, CA 93455-1304
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No. I-03-238
Dear Mr. Benedetti:
QUESTTONS
1. when must the Sam Blakeslee for Assembly Exploratory committee be
terminated?
2. May Mr. Blakeslee loan $100,000 from his personal funds to the Sam Blakeslee
for Assembly Exploratory Committee and an additional amount not to exceed
$100,000 to Friends of Sam Blakeslee, when he controls both committees?
CONCLUSIONS
1. Assuming the Sam Blakeslee for Assembly Exploratory Committee has net debt
outstanding, it must be terminated by March 4,2004,24 months after the March
5,2002, election date.
2. Mr. Blakeslee may loan up to $100,000 to each of his two campaign committees.
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Government Code Section a:i t+; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. bection tSiZelc;1:;1copy
enclosed.)
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the Sam Blakeslee for
Assembly Exploratory Committee for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").I since a portion of your q,re.iio, 
"oncerns 
past
conduct, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to regulation
78329(c).2


File No. I-03-238
Page No. 2
F'ACTS
Sam Blakeslee, a non-incumbent candidate for Assembly, opened an exploratory
committee to run for Assembly in the March 2002 pimary. The exploratory committee
was opened prior to January I , 2001 , and prior to public notification of the results of the
redistricting for Assembly seats. The candidate incurred debt, including a loan from his
personal funds that has not been repaid in full. After redistricting took place, the
incumbent Assembly member holding the seat Mr. Blakeslee sought decided to run for
another term. After this announcement, Mr. Blakeslee decided not to seek election to that
seat. However, his committee remains open to allow for the retirement of debt incurred.
The only debt outstanding is now the candidate's loan to the committee.
Mr. Blakeslee has now formed another committee to run for Assembly. The seat
is in the same Assembly district for which the2002 exploratory committee was opened.
The candidate has loaned money to the new coflrmittee. Combined, Mr. Blakeslee loaned
his committees more than $100,000, although he did not loan more $100,000 to either
one ofhis committees.
AI\ALYSIS
Ouestion I
Section 82007 defines a candidate, in part, as:
"...an individual who ... receives a contribution or makes
an expenditure or gives his or her consent for any other
person to receive a contribution or make an expenditure
with a view to bringing about his or her nomination or
election to any elective office, whether or not the specific
elective office for which he or she will seek nomination or
election is known at the time the contribution is received or
the expenditure is made and whether or not he or she has
announced his or her candidacy or filed a declaration of
candidacy at such time."
Section 82016 states that a candidate controls a committee if he or she:
"...has a significant influence on the actions or decisions of
the committee."
Under these stafutes, Mr. Blakeslee is considered a candidate and the Sam
Blakeslee for Assembly Exploratory Committee is a conkolled committee established to
elect Mr. Blakeslee to the Assembly in the March 2002 election. Candidate controlled
committees organized for elective state office for an election held on or after January 1,
2001, must be terminated under regulation I 8404.1(b)(2). This regulation states, in
relevant part,that:


File No. I-03-238
Page No. 3
"Candidate controlled committees with net debts
outstanding, as definedby 2 Cal. Code Regs. section
18531.6, must be terminated no later than}4 months after
the earliest of the date the candidate is defeated, leaves
office or the term of office for which the committee was
formed ends." (Regulation 18531.6 is enclosed.)
Since Mr. Blakeslee did not win his seat, and assuming the debt his committee
owes meets the definition of 'het debts outstanding," as found in regulation 18531.6, he
must terminate his committee 24 months after the date of the election. Since the election
for which the committee was formed was held March 5,2002, the committee must be
terminated no later than March 4,2004.3 If his committee did not have net debt
outstanding, it was required to terminate within nine months after the date of the election.
(Regulation I 8404. I (bXl ).)
Ouestion 2
Section 85307(b) states:
"A candidate for elective state office may not personally
loan to his or her campaign an amount, the outstanding
balance of which exceeds one hundred thousand dollars
($loo,ooo)."
Regulation 1 8530.8(b) states:
"For purposes of subdivision (b) of Govemment Code
section 85307 and this regulation, 'campaign' encompasses
both the primary and general elections or special and
special runoff elections for a specific term of elective state
office. 'Campaign' includes any of the candidate's
controlled committees formed for the purpose of seeking
that elective state office and all committees formed for the
purpose of supporting the candidate's candidacy for that
elective state office."
Together, section 85307(b) and regulation 18530.8(b) provide that a candidate
may not loan, in the aggregate, more than $100,000 per campaign, which includes both
the primary and general elections to a specific term of office. Regulation 1S530.S(b)
defines the word "campaign" to includ" "*y of the candidate's controlled committees
formed for the purpose of seeking that elective state ffice...." We interpret the words,
"that elective state office," to mean for a specific term to that elective state office, as
specified in the first sentence of the regulation. Thus, a campaign to seek elective state
office in2002 and a campaign to seek the same elective state office in2004 would be
3 Regulation l84}4.l also allows candidates who are continuing to pay outstanding debt to request
an extension of their termination deadline. A copy of the regulation is enclosed.


File No. I-03-238
Page No. 4
considered two separate campaigns, for purposes of this regulation. Mr. Blakeslee,
therefore, may loan up to $100,000 to each of his two committees, one established for the
Assembly race held in 2002 and one for the Assembly race to be held in 2004.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
Moen, PhD
Reform Consultant II
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosures
KSMjg
